sentences.sentence: Looked at in one way, everyone knows what intelligence is; looked at in another way, no one does.
sentences.translation: 从一个角度看，每个人都知道智力是什么；从另一个角度看，却没有人真正知道。
difficult_points: Looked at... 是过去分词短语，表示“从……角度来看”。
分号连接两个对照观点，everyone knows 与 no one does 形成反差。
does 代替前面的 knows what intelligence is，避免重复。

sentences.sentence: In other words, people all have unconscious notions—known as 'implicit theories'—of intelligence, but no one knows for certain what it actually is.
sentences.translation: 换句话说，人们都有关于智力的无意识观念，也就是所谓的“内隐理论”，但没有人确切知道智力实际上是什么。

structure.text: but no one knows for certain what it actually is

difficult_points: In other words 表示作者在重述上一句的悖论。
破折号中的 known as 'implicit theories' 解释 unconscious notions 的术语名称。
what it actually is 是宾语从句，it 指 intelligence。

sentences.sentence: This chapter addresses how people conceptualize intelligence, whatever it may actually be.
sentences.translation: 本章讨论人们如何概念化智力，无论智力本身实际上可能是什么。
difficult_points: addresses 在学术语境中表示“处理、探讨”。
how 引导宾语从句，说明本章关注的具体问题。
whatever it may actually be 表示让步，暂不判定智力的真实本质。

sentences.sentence: But why should we even care what people think intelligence is, as opposed only to valuing whatever it actually is?
sentences.translation: 但我们为什么甚至应该关心人们认为智力是什么，而不只是重视智力实际上是什么呢？

structure.text: But why should we even care
as opposed only to valuing whatever it actually is

difficult_points: But 引出作者对研究意义的疑问。
what people think intelligence is 是 care 的宾语内容。
as opposed to 表示“而不是”，用来对比主观观念和客观本质。

sentences.sentence: There are at least four reasons people's conceptions of intelligence matter.
sentences.translation: 人们对智力的理解之所以重要，至少有四个原因。

structure.text: There are at least four reasons

difficult_points: There are 引出下文将展开的四个理由。
people's conceptions of intelligence matter 是省略 why 的定语从句，修饰 reasons。
matter 作动词，意思是“重要”。

sentences.sentence: First, implicit theories of intelligence drive the way in which people perceive and evaluate their own intelligence and that of others.
sentences.translation: 首先，智力的内隐理论会驱动人们感知和评价自己以及他人智力的方式。

structure.text: in which people perceive and evaluate their own intelligence and that of others

difficult_points: First 标明这是第一个理由。
drive 在这里表示“影响、推动”，不是驾驶。
that of others 中 that 代替 intelligence，避免重复。

sentences.sentence: To better understand the judgments people make about their own and others' abilities, it is useful to learn about people's implicit theories.
sentences.translation: 为了更好地理解人们对自己和他人能力所作的判断，了解人们的内隐理论是有用的。

structure.text: To better understand the judgments people make about their own and others' abilities
to learn about people's implicit theories
people make about their own and others' abilities

difficult_points: 句首 To better understand... 表目的。
the judgments 后省略关系词 that，people make... 修饰 judgments。
it 是形式主语，真正主语是 to learn about people's implicit theories。

sentences.sentence: For example, parents' implicit theories of their children's language development will determine at what ages they will be willing to make various corrections in their children's speech.
sentences.translation: 例如，父母关于孩子语言发展的内隐理论会决定他们愿意在孩子多大时对孩子的言语作出各种纠正。

structure.text: parents' implicit theories of their children's language development
at what ages they will be willing to make various corrections in their children's speech

difficult_points: For example 引出父母语言发展观念这一具体例子。
will determine 表示这些观念会决定后续行为。
at what ages 是嵌入式疑问结构，说明纠正发生的年龄。
make various corrections in... 表示对孩子说话进行纠正。

sentences.sentence: More generally, parents' implicit theories of intelligence will determine at what ages they believe their children are ready to perform various cognitive tasks.
sentences.translation: 更一般地说，父母关于智力的内隐理论会决定他们认为孩子在多大年龄已经准备好完成各种认知任务。

structure.text: at what ages they believe their children are ready to perform various cognitive tasks

difficult_points: More generally 把例子从语言发展扩展到一般智力。
they believe 插入在间接疑问结构中，主语 they 指 parents。
are ready to perform 表示具备完成某事的准备或能力。
cognitive tasks 指涉及思维和认知的任务。

sentences.sentence: Job interviewers will make hiring decisions on the basis of their implicit theories of intelligence.
sentences.translation: 招聘面试官会基于他们关于智力的内隐理论作出录用决定。

structure.text: on the basis of their implicit theories of intelligence

difficult_points: Job interviewers 是作出判断的人群例子。
make hiring decisions 表示作出招聘或录用决定。
on the basis of 表示这些决定所依据的标准。

sentences.sentence: People will decide who to be friends with on the basis of such theories.
sentences.translation: 人们还会基于这类理论来决定和谁做朋友。

structure.text: on the basis of such theories

difficult_points: who to be friends with 是疑问词加不定式结构，作 decide 的宾语。
such theories 指前文的 implicit theories of intelligence。
on the basis of 再次强调观念会成为日常判断依据。

sentences.sentence: In sum, knowledge about implicit theories of intelligence is important because this knowledge is so often used by people to make judgments in the course of their everyday lives.
sentences.translation: 总之，关于智力内隐理论的知识很重要，因为人们在日常生活过程中经常使用这种知识来作出判断。

structure.text: knowledge about implicit theories of intelligence
because this knowledge is so often used by people to make judgments in the course of their everyday lives

difficult_points: In sum 表示对第一点的总结。
this knowledge 回指关于内隐理论的知识。
is so often used 是被动语态，说明这种知识在日常判断中被频繁使用。
in the course of 表示“在……过程中”。

sentences.sentence: Second, the implicit theories of scientific investigators ultimately give rise to their explicit theories.
sentences.translation: 第二，科学研究者的内隐理论最终会产生他们的显性理论。

structure.text: the implicit theories of scientific investigators
ultimately give rise to their explicit theories

difficult_points: Second 标明第二个理由。
scientific investigators 指科学研究者。
give rise to 表示导致或产生，说明内隐理论会影响显性理论。

sentences.sentence: Thus it is useful to find out what these implicit theories are.
sentences.translation: 因此，弄清这些内隐理论是什么是有用的。

structure.text: to find out what these implicit theories are

difficult_points: Thus 表示从上一句推出结论。
it 是形式主语，真正主语是 to find out...。
what these implicit theories are 是 find out 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: Implicit theories provide a framework that is useful in defining the general scope of a phenomenon—especially a not-well-understood phenomenon.
sentences.translation: 内隐理论提供了一个框架，有助于界定某一现象的一般范围，尤其是一个尚未被充分理解的现象。

structure.text: that is useful in defining the general scope of a phenomenon

difficult_points: that 引导定语从句，修饰 framework。
useful in defining 表示在界定某事方面有用。
破折号后的 especially... 对 phenomenon 作进一步限定。
not-well-understood 是复合形容词，表示尚未被充分理解。

sentences.sentence: These implicit theories can suggest what aspects of the phenomenon have been more or less attended to in previous investigations.
sentences.translation: 这些内隐理论能够提示，在以往研究中，该现象的哪些方面或多或少受到过关注。

structure.text: what aspects of the phenomenon have been more or less attended to in previous investigations

difficult_points: what aspects... 是 suggest 的宾语从句。
have been attended to 是现在完成时被动语态，表示已被关注。
more or less 表示程度不一。
previous investigations 指以往研究。

sentences.sentence: Third, implicit theories can be useful when an investigator suspects that existing explicit theories are wrong or misleading.
sentences.translation: 第三，当研究者怀疑现有显性理论错误或具有误导性时，内隐理论可能会有用。

structure.text: when an investigator suspects that existing explicit theories are wrong or misleading

difficult_points: Third 标明第三个理由。
when 引导时间或条件状语从句，说明内隐理论发挥作用的情境。
that existing explicit theories are wrong or misleading 是 suspects 的宾语从句。

sentences.sentence: If an investigation of implicit theories reveals little correspondence between the extant implicit and explicit theories, the implicit theories may be wrong.
sentences.translation: 如果一项关于内隐理论的研究显示，现存的内隐理论与显性理论之间几乎没有对应关系，那么内隐理论可能是错的。

structure.text: If an investigation of implicit theories reveals little correspondence between the extant implicit and explicit theories
the implicit theories may be wrong

difficult_points: If 引导条件状语从句，提出判断内隐理论错误的一种情况。
little correspondence 表示几乎没有对应或一致关系。
extant 表示“现存的”。
may be wrong 是谨慎推断，而非绝对结论。

sentences.sentence: But the possibility also needs to be taken into account that the explicit theories are wrong and in need of correction or supplementation.
sentences.translation: 但是，也需要考虑另一种可能性：显性理论是错误的，需要修正或补充。

structure.text: But the possibility also needs to be taken into account
that the explicit theories are wrong and in need of correction or supplementation
in need of correction or supplementation

difficult_points: But 引出与上一句相反的另一种解释。
needs to be taken into account 是被动结构，表示“需要被考虑”。
that 从句解释 possibility 的具体内容。
correction or supplementation 表示修正或补充。

sentences.sentence: For example, some implicit theories of intelligence suggest the need for expansion of some of our explicit theories of the construct.
sentences.translation: 例如，某些关于智力的内隐理论表明，我们的一些关于这一构念的显性理论需要扩展。

structure.text: of some of our explicit theories of the construct

difficult_points: For example 引出对上一句“显性理论需要补充”的例证。
the need for expansion 表示需要扩大理论覆盖范围。
the construct 指 intelligence 这一理论构念。

sentences.sentence: Finally, understanding implicit theories of intelligence can help elucidate developmental and cross-cultural differences.
sentences.translation: 最后，理解智力的内隐理论有助于阐明发展差异和跨文化差异。

structure.text: can help elucidate developmental and cross-cultural differences

difficult_points: Finally 标明第四个理由。
understanding... 是动名词短语作主语。
developmental and cross-cultural differences 指发展阶段差异和文化差异。

sentences.sentence: As mentioned earlier, people have expectations for intellectual performances that differ for children of different ages.
sentences.translation: 如前所述，人们对智力表现有不同期待，而这些期待会因儿童年龄不同而不同。

structure.text: that differ for children of different ages

difficult_points: As mentioned earlier 表示回指前文内容。
expectations for intellectual performances 指对智力表现的期待。
that differ... 修饰 expectations，而不是 performances。

sentences.sentence: How these expectations differ is in part a function of culture.
sentences.translation: 这些期待如何不同，在一定程度上取决于文化。

structure.text: is in part a function of culture

difficult_points: How these expectations differ 是名词性从句作主语。
in part 表示“在一定程度上”，不是全部原因。
a function of culture 表示受文化影响或由文化决定。

sentences.sentence: For example, expectations for children who participate in Western-style schooling are almost certain to be different from those for children who do not participate in such schooling.
sentences.translation: 例如，对接受西式学校教育的儿童的期待，几乎肯定不同于对不接受这种学校教育的儿童的期待。

structure.text: expectations for children who participate in Western-style schooling
are almost certain to be different from those
who do not participate in such schooling

difficult_points: 两个 who 从句分别修饰两类 children。
those 代替 expectations，避免重复。
are almost certain to 表示高度确定的判断。
such schooling 指 Western-style schooling。

sentences.sentence: I have suggested that there are three major implicit theories of how intelligence relates to society as a whole (Sternberg, 1997).
sentences.translation: 我曾提出，关于智力如何与整个社会相关，有三种主要的内隐理论（Sternberg，1997）。

structure.text: that there are three major implicit theories of how intelligence relates to society as a whole
how intelligence relates to society as a whole

difficult_points: have suggested 是现在完成时，表示作者此前提出过该观点。
that 从句说明提出的具体内容。
how intelligence relates to society as a whole 修饰 theories 的研究主题。
括号是文献引用，应保留为原文信息。

expressions.text: relates to society as a whole

sentences.sentence: These might be called Hamiltonian, Jeffersonian, and Jacksonian.
sentences.translation: 这些理论可以称为汉密尔顿式、杰斐逊式和杰克逊式。
difficult_points: These 指上一句提到的三种主要内隐理论。
might be called 用被动语态和 might 表示命名上的谨慎。
三个 -ian 形容词对应三位美国政治人物的思想传统。

sentences.sentence: These views are not based strictly, but rather, loosely, on the philosophies of Alexander Hamilton, Thomas Jefferson, and Andrew Jackson, three great statesmen in the history of the United States.
sentences.translation: 这些观点并不是严格地，而是较为宽泛地，以亚历山大·汉密尔顿、托马斯·杰斐逊和安德鲁·杰克逊的哲学为基础；这三人是美国历史上的伟大政治家。

structure.text: are not based strictly, but rather, loosely, on the philosophies of Alexander Hamilton, Thomas Jefferson, and Andrew Jackson
three great statesmen in the history of the United States

difficult_points: not... but rather... 表示不是严格依据，而是松散参照。
are based on 是被动结构，说明命名依据。
three great statesmen... 是同位语，补充说明三位人物。

sentences.sentence: The Hamiltonian view, which is similar to the Platonic view, is that people are born with different levels of intelligence and that those who are less intelligent need the good offices of the more intelligent to keep them in line, whether they are called government officials or, in Plato's term, philosopher-kings.
sentences.translation: 汉密尔顿式观点类似于柏拉图式观点，它认为人生来具有不同的智力水平，而智力较低者需要智力较高者的善意帮助来约束他们，无论这些较高智力者被称为政府官员，还是用柏拉图的话说，被称为哲人王。

structure.text: which is similar to the Platonic view
is that people are born with different levels of intelligence and that those who are less intelligent need the good offices of the more intelligent to keep them in line
whether they are called government officials or, in Plato's term, philosopher-kings

difficult_points: which 从句补充说明 Hamiltonian view 与 Platonic view 的相似性。
is that... 后接两个并列 that 从句，解释该观点内容。
those who are less intelligent 指智力较低的人，who 从句修饰 those。
to keep them in line 表示使他们守规矩或受约束。

sentences.sentence: Herrnstein and Murray (1994) seem to have shared this belief when they wrote about the emergence of a cognitive (high-IQ) elite, which eventually would have to take responsibility for the largely irresponsible masses of non-elite (low-IQ) people who cannot take care of themselves.
sentences.translation: 赫恩斯坦和默里（1994）在写到认知（高智商）精英的出现时，似乎也持有这种信念；这种精英最终将不得不为大量基本上不负责任、无法照顾自己的非精英（低智商）人群承担责任。

structure.text: seem to have shared this belief
when they wrote about the emergence of a cognitive (high-IQ) elite
which eventually would have to take responsibility for the largely irresponsible masses of non-elite (low-IQ) people who cannot take care of themselves

difficult_points: seem to have shared 是对过去态度的推测。
this belief 指上一句 Hamiltonian view 的核心信念。
which 指 a cognitive (high-IQ) elite，不是 emergence。
who cannot take care of themselves 修饰 non-elite people。

sentences.sentence: Left to themselves, the unintelligent would create, as they always have created, a kind of chaos.
sentences.translation: 如果任其自行其是，不够聪明的人会制造出某种混乱，就像他们一直以来所做的那样。
difficult_points: Left to themselves 是过去分词短语，表示如果无人管理或干预。
the unintelligent 是“the + 形容词”结构，泛指智力较低的人。
as they always have created 插入说明作者归纳的历史惯例。
would create 表示该观点下的假设性结果。

sentences.sentence: The Jeffersonian view is that people should have equal opportunities, but they do not necessarily avail themselves equally of these opportunities and are not necessarily equally rewarded for their accomplishments.
sentences.translation: 杰斐逊式观点认为，人们应当拥有平等机会，但他们未必会同等地利用这些机会，也未必会因其成就而得到同等回报。

structure.text: is that people should have equal opportunities
but they do not necessarily avail themselves equally of these opportunities
and are not necessarily equally rewarded for their accomplishments

difficult_points: is that... 引出该观点的具体内容。
avail themselves of 表示利用机会，是固定搭配。
not necessarily 表示“不一定”，并不否定所有情况。
are rewarded for 是被动结构，说明回报依据成就。

sentences.sentence: People are rewarded for what they accomplish, if given equal opportunity.
sentences.translation: 如果被给予平等机会，人们会因他们所完成的事情而得到回报。

structure.text: are rewarded for what they accomplish

difficult_points: are rewarded 是被动语态，强调回报被给予。
what they accomplish 是介词 for 的宾语从句。
if given equal opportunity 是省略结构，完整含义是 if they are given equal opportunity。

expressions.meaning_zh: 在被给予平等机会的情况下

sentences.sentence: Low achievers are not rewarded to the same extent as high achievers.
sentences.translation: 低成就者不会像高成就者那样获得同等程度的回报。

structure.text: to the same extent as high achievers

difficult_points: Low achievers 与 high achievers 形成对比。
are not rewarded 是被动语态。
to the same extent as 表示达到相同程度。

sentences.sentence: In the Jeffersonian view, the goal of education is not to favor or foster an elite, as in the Hamiltonian tradition, but rather to allow children the opportunities to make full use of the skills they have.
sentences.translation: 在杰斐逊式观点中，教育的目标不是像汉密尔顿传统那样偏袒或培养精英，而是让孩子有机会充分运用他们已有的技能。

structure.text: is not to favor or foster an elite
but rather to allow children the opportunities to make full use of the skills they have

difficult_points: not... but rather... 明确对比两种教育目标。
favor or foster an elite 指偏袒或培养精英。
as in the Hamiltonian tradition 是插入比较，说明被否定的传统。
they have 是省略关系词的定语从句，修饰 skills。

sentences.sentence: My own views are similar to these (Sternberg, 1997).
sentences.translation: 我自己的观点与这些观点相似（Sternberg，1997）。
difficult_points: My own views 指作者自己的立场。
these 回指前文 Jeffersonian view 的相关观点。
括号为文献引用，表明作者此前已有相关论述。

sentences.sentence: The Jacksonian view is that all people are equal, not only as human beings but in terms of their competencies—that one person would serve as well as another in government or on a jury or in almost any position of responsibility.
sentences.translation: 杰克逊式观点认为，所有人都是平等的，不仅作为人是平等的，而且在能力方面也是平等的——也就是说，一个人在政府、陪审团或几乎任何责任岗位上都能和另一个人一样胜任。

structure.text: is that all people are equal
not only as human beings but in terms of their competencies
that one person would serve as well as another in government or on a jury or in almost any position of responsibility

difficult_points: is that... 引出 Jacksonian view 的核心命题。
not only... but... 说明平等既是人的平等，也是能力层面的平等。
破折号后的 that 从句进一步解释 competencies 上平等的含义。
serve as well as another 表示与另一个人同样胜任。

sentences.sentence: In this view of democracy, people are essentially intersubstitutable except for specialized skills, all of which can be learned.
sentences.translation: 在这种民主观中，除了专门技能之外，人们本质上可以相互替代，而所有这些专门技能都可以习得。

structure.text: are essentially intersubstitutable except for specialized skills
all of which can be learned

difficult_points: In this view 指 Jacksonian view 下的民主观。
intersubstitutable 表示可以相互替代。
except for specialized skills 表示除专门技能外。
all of which 指 specialized skills，说明这些技能可学习。

sentences.sentence: In this view, we do not need or want any institutions that might lead to favoring one group over another.
sentences.translation: 在这种观点中，我们不需要也不想要任何可能导致偏袒某一群体而非另一群体的制度。

structure.text: do not need or want any institutions
that might lead to favoring one group over another

difficult_points: do not need or want 并列否定两个动词。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 institutions。
lead to 后接动名词 favoring。
one group over another 表示偏向某一群体而非另一群体。

expressions.meaning_zh: 某一群体优于另一群体

sentences.sentence: Implicit theories of intelligence and of the relationship of intelligence to society perhaps need to be considered more carefully than they have been because they often serve as underlying presuppositions for explicit theories and even experimental designs that are then taken as scientific contributions.
sentences.translation: 关于智力以及智力与社会关系的内隐理论也许需要比以往更仔细地加以考虑，因为它们常常充当显性理论甚至实验设计的潜在预设，而这些显性理论和实验设计随后会被视为科学贡献。

structure.text: Implicit theories of intelligence and of the relationship of intelligence to society
perhaps need to be considered more carefully than they have been
because they often serve as underlying presuppositions for explicit theories and even experimental designs
that are then taken as scientific contributions

difficult_points: 主语由两个 of 短语扩展，分别涉及智力本身和智力与社会的关系。
than they have been 省略 considered，表示比过去更仔细地考虑。
because 从句解释为什么内隐理论重要。
that are then taken as scientific contributions 修饰 explicit theories and experimental designs。

sentences.sentence: Until scholars are able to discuss their implicit theories and thus their assumptions, they are likely to miss the point of what others are saying when discussing their explicit theories and their data.
sentences.translation: 在学者能够讨论自己的内隐理论以及由此产生的假设之前，他们很可能会误解他人在讨论其显性理论和数据时所表达的要点。

structure.text: Until scholars are able to discuss their implicit theories and thus their assumptions
they are likely to miss the point
of what others are saying when discussing their explicit theories and their data

difficult_points: Until 引导时间状语从句，说明在某一条件实现之前会出现的问题。
thus their assumptions 表示内隐理论背后的假设也需要被讨论。
are likely to 表示很可能发生。
when discussing... 是省略结构，完整含义是 when others are discussing...。
